ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic toast entry (FOODON_00002683) but no specific entry for multigrain thick toast with pork floss and condensed milk. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E covers bread and similar products; no specific code exists for multigrain floss thick toast. Closest parent category used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific composite Singaporean toast product.

serving.description: 1 serving (2 slices thick toast with floss and condensed milk)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving consists of two thick slices of multigrain toast topped with pork floss and a drizzle of condensed milk, weighing approximately 130 g.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: HPB Singapore Foodo multigrain bread, pork floss (rousong), and condensed milk nutrient data
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) Foodo / USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Foodo entry exists for multigrain floss thick toast as a composite dish. Values estimated from weighted component contributions: multigrain bread (~80 g per 100 g final product basis), pork floss rousong (~12 g), and condensed milk (~8 g), using HPB Foodo entries for multigrain bread and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for pork floss (USDA NDB 07060) and condensed milk (USDA NDB 01009). Proportions reflect typical Toast Box-style preparation. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of multigrain bread and composite toast products — analogous estimate based on multigrain bread GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for multigrain floss thick toast. GI estimated at 65 based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for multigrain bread (GI range 43–69, mean ~55–65 depending on formulation). The addition of pork floss (protein, low GI contribution) and condensed milk (moderate sugar, GI ~60) results in a composite GI estimated at 65 (medium). Glycemic load per 130 g serving calculated as: (65 × 52.4 × 1.30) / 100 = 44.5 g. This is a high glycemic load per serving due to the large portion of refined and multigrain carbohydrates.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from pork floss
hypertension.guidance: Pork floss contributes approximately 420 mg sodium per 100 g; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one slice and avoid adding additional salty condiments such as soy sauce or kaya with salt.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load and added sugar from condensed milk
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic load of approximately 44.5 per serving and 11.2 g sugar per 100 g largely from condensed milk, this toast is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes without significant portion reduction and substitution of condensed milk with a low-sugar alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and sodium from pork floss and condensed milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of 2.6 g saturated fat and 420 mg sodium per 100 g warrants moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease; choosing a smaller portion and requesting reduced condensed milk can help lower both saturated fat and sugar intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Multigrain Floss Thick Toast is a popular Singaporean kopitiam-style breakfast and snack item served at chains such as Toast Box. It consists of two thick slices of multigrain bread — made from a blend of whole grains including wheat, oats, and seeds — that are toasted until golden and crisp on the outside while remaining soft inside. The toast is generously topped with a layer of pork floss (rousong), a dried, fluffy, savoury meat product made from slow-cooked and shredded pork, and finished with a drizzle of sweetened condensed milk, which adds a creamy, sweet contrast to the salty floss. The flavour profile is a distinctive sweet-savoury combination characteristic of Singaporean toast culture. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate protein (~10.8 g/100 g) from the pork floss and bread, significant carbohydrates (~52.4 g/100 g) primarily from the multigrain bread, and notable sodium (~420 mg/100 g) from the pork floss. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), but the glycemic load per serving is high (~44.5) due to the large carbohydrate portion. The dish is not suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension without careful portion control. It is not halal-certified in standard formulations due to the use of pork floss. The multigrain bread base provides dietary fibre (~3.1 g/100 g), B vitamins, and iron, offering modest nutritional advantages over white bread toast variants.

What is pork floss toast Singapore?
Glycemic index of multigrain toast with condensed milk
Sodium content of pork floss toast
meal_context: light meal paired with kopi or teh

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for multigrain floss thick toast as a composite dish. Nutrient values were estimated from weighted component contributions using HPB Foodo (multigrain bread) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (pork floss, condensed milk). GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database multigrain bread entries. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from component analysis and should be verified against a direct laboratory analysis or an official composite dish entry if one becomes available in HPB Foodo or a regional food composition database. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the component source databases for this food type. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the component source databases for this food type. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the component source databases for this food type. Vitamin C is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero) as multigrain bread, pork floss, and condensed milk contain negligible or no vitamin C per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. This dish is not halal-certified in standard formulations due to the use of pork floss (rousong). The pork floss topping is the primary driver of sodium content. Condensed milk is the primary driver of added sugar. GI estimated at 65 based on University of Sydney GI Database multigrain bread analogous entries; glycemic load per 130 g serving is approximately 44.5, which is high. Allergen note: gluten is present from bread; milk is present from condensed milk; soy may be present in some pork floss formulations.

Iteration 2: Self-scored all 8 criteria and 6 hard gates; all pass. Final decision PASS confirmed.
